name: a retrospective 'I left Acme in March' should close the open employment interval, not conflict
description: SPEC (docs/reference/ANALYSIS.md 'state ... The old fact stays true about its interval' + SCD-2): note A opens an OPEN employer interval (Tomas worksFor Acme since 2023, no end, value_json {state:current}). Note B arriving now ('Tomas left Acme back in March 2026', value_json {state:ended, end:2026-03}) supplies the END of that SAME relationship — a valid_to backfill on the still-open current state, with the same object entity (Acme) and same valid_from (2023). The spec-correct outcome is ONE Acme fact whose interval is now closed (it stays true about its interval) with NO fact_conflict review — nobody disputes Tomas worked at Acme; we merely learned when it ended. GAP (closed): supersession.decide used to have no 'close interval in place' path — value_json differed so values_equal was False, and with equal valid_from the candidate (later reported_at) won the >= test, chained a second Acme row, and filed a spurious fact_conflict. decide()._interval_close now recognizes an end-date backfill on the same object/valid_from as an in-place interval close (Decision.close_id/close_valid_to, applied by the pipeline as an UPDATE on the open row).

steps.body: Tomas has worked at Acme since 2023.
I should note Tomas actually left Acme back in March.

facts.statement: Tomas worked for Acme from 2023 until March 2026.
